name: Makua Beach (Pray for Sex Beach)
tagline: The end of the road on the Waianae Coast — remote, raw, and full of spinner dolphins.
parking: Dirt pulloff along Farrington Hwy; limited space
hours: Open during daylight; road may close for military exercises (check Makua Beach access schedule)
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) for calm water; early morning for spinner dolphin pods in the bay
talkStory: Makua is one of the most remote beaches you can drive to on Oahu. The snorkeling is best along the rocky edges of the bay where you will find healthy coral and less boat traffic. Spinner dolphins frequently come into the bay in the mornings — stay 50 yards back as federal law protects them. The beach is sometimes closed for live-fire military training at Makua Valley so check access before driving all the way out. No facilities, no lifeguard, no shade — bring everything you need.
